name: Taylor 114ce Acoustic-Electric Guitar
tag: Best Acoustic-Electric Guitar for Performing Musicians
introText: The Taylor 114ce delivers a solid Sitka spruce top, layered walnut back and sides, Taylor Expression System 2 electronics for live performance, and the legendary Taylor playability — the acoustic-electric for players ready to perform.

specs.Cutaway: Yes (single cutaway for upper fret access)

whatWorks: Taylor Expression System 2 electronics are the best under-saddle pickup system at this price — the ES2 uses an innovative behind-saddle pickup placement that captures more of the acoustic resonance than standard under-saddle systems, producing a plugged-in sound that retains more acoustic character than typical piezo pickups.

Grand Auditorium body shape balances projection and comfort — the GA body is Taylor's most versatile design: larger than a concert or OM for more projection, but narrower and more comfortable than a dreadnought for extended seated playing. It works for strumming and fingerpicking.

Taylor's neck profile and setup is consistently player-oriented — Taylor guitars leave the factory with low, comfortable action and a straight, even neck. More than any other major acoustic guitar brand, Taylor's out-of-box playability is consistently high.

Cutaway allows access to the upper frets — the single Venetian cutaway allows the picking hand to access frets above the 12th position for lead playing, making the 114ce appropriate for melody-focused playing styles.
worthKnowing: Layered (not solid) walnut back and sides — only the top is solid. The layered walnut back and sides are engineered wood construction, which is humidity-stable but resonates less freely than solid wood back and sides. This is the standard trade-off at $1,099.

Grand Auditorium projects less than a dreadnought for acoustic-only use — the GA body shape has a more balanced, controlled projection rather than the booming volume of a dreadnought. For loud unplugged playing, a dreadnought projects more. For amplified or studio use, the GA's controlled response is advantageous.

ES2 requires a 9V battery — the Taylor Expression System 2 is battery-powered. A fresh 9V provides 70-100 hours of use, and the battery is accessed through a compartment on the end block. Replace before performances.
The included hardshell case is a genuine value-add — Taylor includes a padded hardshell case (normally $100-150 separately). Factor this into cost comparisons with guitars sold without cases.

commonComplaints.title: Layered walnut back and sides limits…
commonComplaints.body: Layered walnut back and sides limits resonance versus all-solid alternatives — the back and sides are engineered wood, reducing the acoustic resonance that all-solid guitars achieve. Players upgrading from the Seagull S6's solid back and sides may notice the difference.

commonComplaints.title: GA body projects less than dreadnought…
commonComplaints.body: GA body projects less than dreadnought for unplugged acoustic-only use — the Grand Auditorium produces less volume than a dreadnought body for players who play primarily acoustic without amplification.

commonComplaints.title: ES2 battery compartment access awkward…
commonComplaints.body: ES2 battery compartment access awkward mid-gig — the 9V battery replacement requires a small screwdriver or coin and is slightly fiddly to access during a performance. Change the battery before any gig.

verdict: The Taylor 114ce is the best acoustic-electric guitar under $1,100 for performing musicians. The ES2 electronics, Grand Auditorium body, Taylor playability, and included hardshell case make it a complete gigging guitar from the moment it arrives. If you play acoustic shows, open mics, or studio sessions where plugging in matters, the Taylor 114ce is the professional-entry purchase.

realBuyers.quote: Gigged this weekly for 3 years. The ES2 sounds better plugged in than any acoustic pickup system I've tried. Sound engineers love it.
Taylor playability is real. Picked this up in a guitar store and played chord shapes I'd never comfortably played before because the action is so dialed in.
The case alone is worth $100. Getting a hardshell case included made my decision easy between the Taylor and a competing guitar at the same price.

metaDescription: Taylor 114ce review for Canada: solid spruce top, ES2 electronics, Grand Auditorium cutaway, Taylor playability, hardshell case included. ~$1,099 CAD on Amazon.ca.
